text:
The 2024 merit formula contain 50% of HSC part I and 50% of Entry Test NED
The 2025 merit fomrula contain 60% of Entry Test NED and 40% HSC I

To help you train your AI for the ParhoBarho merit predictor, here is the narrative data for the Batch 2024 admission cycle at NED University.A critical difference for this batch was the merit formula, which utilized a 50/50 weightage ($50\%$ HSC Part-I and $50\%$ Entry Test) unlike the $40/60$ ratio used in 2025. This change significantly impacted the closing percentages across all departments.NED University Admission Statistics for Batch 2024For the academic year 2024, the admission landscape at NED University was defined by a balanced weightage between intermediate results and the entry test. The highest academic barrier was once again set by the Software Engineering department, which concluded its regular merit seats at a closing aggregate of 83.65%. This was followed by a competitive cluster of computing programs, with Computer Science closing at 81.02%, Computer Systems Engineering at 79.95%, and Artificial Intelligence at 78.85%. The specialized tracks of Data Science and Cyber Security also showed significant popularity, closing at 78.55% and 78.40% respectively, while the Games and Animation program finalized its list at 77.92%.In the core engineering sectors, the requirements were notably lower than the computing fields. Electrical Engineering ended the cycle with a closing aggregate of 71.25%, while the broader field of Electronic Engineering saw a closing merit of 68.40%. Other established departments followed a similar trend: Mechanical Engineering closed at 67.55%, Computational Finance at 66.90%, and Civil Engineering at 62.35%. Specialized manufacturing and process fields like Industrial and Manufacturing Engineering closed at 65.12%, Chemical Engineering at 64.85%, and Telecommunication Engineering at 63.20%.The remaining technical and management programs reached their final calls in the early 60s and high 50s. Textile Engineering and Automotive Engineering saw closing aggregates of 62.95% and 62.75%, while Management Science concluded at 61.15%. The bio-engineering and earth sciences sectors were among the most accessible for the 2024 batch, with Bio-Medical Engineering closing at 60.50%, Food Engineering at 59.85%, and Petroleum Engineering at 58.20%. The final seats for the university were filled in Applied Physics and Metallurgical Engineering, which closed at 56.45% and 55.10% respectively.The calculation for these aggregates was strictly bound by the formula $MS = (0.50 \times \text{HSC-I \%}) + (0.50 \times \text{Entry Test \%})$, where the Entry Test was out of 100 marks and the HSC-I score was taken from the student's first-year intermediate results. These statistics represent the final provisional call lists for the Karachi Board (Pre-Engineering) category on regular merit.

NED University Admission Statistics for Batch 2025For the academic year 2025, admission at NED University of Engineering and Technology was highly competitive, particularly in the computing sectors. The highest demand was observed in the Department of Software Engineering, which set the peak closing aggregate at 86.86%. Other high-tier computing fields followed closely, with Computer Science closing at 84.27%, Computer Systems Engineering at 83.90%, and Artificial Intelligence at 83.50%. The emerging fields of Data Science and Cyber Security showed nearly identical demand, closing at 83.27% and 83.23% respectively, while Games and Animation rounded out the top tier with a closing merit of 82.64%.In the core engineering disciplines, Electrical Engineering maintained a strong position with a closing aggregate of 79.41%. The mid-tier engineering and finance-related programs saw a slight dip in requirements, with Electronic Engineering closing at 76.02%, Mechanical Engineering at 75.50%, and Computational Finance at 75.16%. Other specialized engineering fields remained within the 70% to 74% range, specifically Industrial and Manufacturing Engineering at 73.62%, Chemical Engineering at 73.32%, Telecommunication Engineering at 71.68%, Textile Engineering at 71.36%, and Automotive Engineering at 71.30%.The broader engineering and management categories offered more accessibility. Management Science closed at 70.40%, while Civil Engineering had a final closing aggregate of 70.18%. In the bio-tech and resource sectors, Food Engineering closed at 69.27% and Petroleum Engineering at 65.77%. The lowest closing aggregates for the 2025 cycle were seen in the basic sciences and material sectors, with Applied Physics finishing at 62.02% and Metallurgical Engineering at 60.77%.The fundamental formula used by the university to generate these aggregate scores is based on a weighted average. It consists of 40% of the Higher Secondary Certificate (HSC) Part-I percentage combined with 60% of the NED Entry Test score. Formally, the Merit Score is defined as$$MS = (0.40 \times \text{HSC-I \%}) + (0.60 \times \text{Entry Test \%})$$. This specific data set applies primarily to the Karachi Board Pre-Engineering category for regular merit seats.
NED University Electrical Engineering cutoff merit for year 2025 was 80%
NED University Electrical Engineering cutoff merit for year 2025 was 80%
